title: What Is a trusted Eva source
display_title: What Is a trusted Eva source
wikitext:
[]: What Is a trusted Eva source?             Quote Post by EvaCub » Wed Sep 19, 2007 10:26 am Ok so Ive noticed a lot recently that there is a lot of info/theories and sources about Evangelion Besides the word straight from anno's mouth how can you seperate true from false are can you?

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Wed Sep 19, 2007 10:57 am From personal experience, any question you have about the authenticness of ANYTHING Eva related (whether it be a website or just some crazy theory you have) can be posed here and you'll be told pretty quick. As for anything just straight up being true or false, I don't think there's any kind of dead giveaway to it. Anything said on 4Chan is 110% true, follow it and be enlightened not really...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 19, 2007 11:38 am I think we've collected together a good group of people with quite a lot knowledge of this show. Considering other forums and communities out there that are active, I think we're the closest thing to an "authority" on Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Wed Sep 19, 2007 11:44 am Sometimes I just don't know who to trust but Yes I do think that Here at Evamonkey is the best place to find info and validate stuff. I guess what I was really asking was besides evamonkey... But Evamonkey Is great, not saying it isnt this is the only place i can get into anything on the deeper side of evangelion it seems @Evamonkey thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 19, 2007 12:26 pm I would say Rachel's EvaCommentary.Org, but it's largely the same group of people. It seems to me like the Eva community in terms of actual websites and forums has shrunk to only a few, and EMF seems to be on top. Trivial Being has quite a bit of activity, but I prefer to post here primarily. The Eva ML has some knowledgeable people, but they seem to snub their noses at a lot of topics of discussion. Anime Nation Forums used to have the strongest Eva board, but then a few things happened that drove the active users away, many of whom ended up here.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Sep 19, 2007 2:35 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think we've collected together a good group of people with quite a lot knowledge of this show. Considering other forums and communities out there that are active, I think we're the closest thing to an "authority" on Eva.

[]: Re: What Is a trusted Eva source?             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Sep 19, 2007 3:53 pm EvaCub wrote: Besides the word straight from anno's mouth how can you seperate true from false are can you?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 19, 2007 4:36 pm CitizenGeek wrote: I don't like how a lot of people pass off theories as facts around here

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 19, 2007 6:24 pm I've never really understood the attitude of some of the people at the EML, but anyway. I agree that both this forum, Anime Nation Forum (though a lot of the good threads are taken down now, I think?? I know Evacommentary.org has backups of the threads, so...) and the currently-in-process of being reborn Eva Commentary Fan Geeks Project ("Wanna be a member? Wanna be a member?") are very good sources for finding out things when it comes to Eva. Personally, I've learned quite a lot about Eva here, which is part of the reason I joined this particular forum to begin with (even though a place like, say, ANF, is probably equally good). But yeah. When the majority of fans here say something is fact, such as Rei=Lilith, you can be reassured that that IS the case.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Sep 19, 2007 6:40 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: When the majority of fans here say something is fact, such as Rei=Lilith, you can be reassured that that IS the case.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 19, 2007 6:45 pm ....Even if it's backed up by facts/evidence from the series itself? That's what I meant by that statement.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:01 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: ....Even if it's backed up by facts/evidence from the series itself? That's what I meant by that statement.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:21 pm BrikHaus wrote: I'm not arguing Rei=Lilith, but just because the majority believes something, that does not necessarily make it the truth.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:24 pm DatDude wrote: IE Lilith is female, Adam is like Liilth, Therefore Adam is female

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:31 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: DatDude wrote: IE Lilith is female, Adam is like Liilth, Therefore Adam is female

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:35 pm @ Datdude & SEELE08: You're talking about Inductive Reasoning, which isn't necessarily always true (the Ray Charles=God example is a good one to make that point). From what I've seen here, Deductive Reasoning is what's used to figure things out. Such as Adam is Female, like Lilith. Since Kaworu refereed to Adam as "Our Mother" and all. This does NOT mean, however, that Kaworu himself is female; although he is-in a way-his own Mother, since he has Adam's soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:41 pm I feel like it's time for another priceless Reichu quote, if you're looking for empirical evidence you're watching the wrong show . It would have been a much better arguement if he wasn't a vocal opponent of that theory. You certainly can't think we're like the horrid examples you made.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:49 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: @ Datdude & SEELE08: You're talking about Inductive Reasoning, which isn't necessarily always true (the Ray Charles=God example is a good one to make that point). From what I've seen here, Deductive Reasoning is what's used to figure things out. Such as Adam is Female, like Lilith. Since Kaworu refereed to Adam as "Our Mother" and all. This does NOT mean, however, that Kaworu himself is female; although he is-in a way-his own Mother, since he has Adam's soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:54 pm I'm not saying I was necessarily using deductive reasoning just now. But the fact that Adam is refereed to as "Mother", NOT "Father", NOT "Progenitor of life", NOT "Parent", obviously means something. @ AEF: Apparently, the quote Reichuism wrote: But I should warn you NGE is not designed to be an open-ended "choose-your-own-adventure". Anno-tachi are telling an extremely defined story, and it will only make sense in entirety if you manage to figure out its rules, play by them without discrimination, and fit EVERYthing together in a logical and internally consistent manner. Which is one horrendous task, obviously. If the above is something you already know, then pay it no mind. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Sep 19, 2007 7:55 pm SEELE 08 wrote: But that is inductive, you're saying Lilith is female, therefore another angel must be female, due to one phrase in an admitted plot hole!
